label: Hotel Late Check-In Credit Review
customer_problem: A guest arriving after 10 PM says the room was not ready last time and asks for a guaranteed suite upgrade plus a room credit.
customer_facts: The guest expects late arrival support.
The guest connects the request to a previous room readiness issue.
The guest asks for a suite upgrade and room credit.
risk_flags: Do not guarantee a suite upgrade.
Do not say the room credit is already approved.
Keep goodwill credits above $75 routed to the duty manager.
success_criteria: Ask for or mention the late check-in reservation note before 9 PM.
Explain that room-credit requests need stay-record verification.
Close with the duty manager or front desk ownership path.
operator_notes: Good for showing polite refusal without a hard no.
The reply should preserve a hospitality voice while staying bounded.
